figures.caption: Figure 1: Comparison of VL-SAM/VL-SAM-v2 and VL-SAM-v3. VL-SAM-v3 extends previous variants with retrieval-grounded visual priors and Memory-Guided Prompt Refinement.
Table 1: Zero-shot open-vocabulary object detection results on LVIS minival and val.
Table 2: Zero-shot open-ended object detection results on LVIS minival.

Figure 2: Overview of VL-SAM-v3. (a) Scene-aware visual memory construction from groundingstyle data. (b) Retrieval-augmented open-world detection, where retrieved visual evidence is converted into sparse and dense visual priors and injected into the detector through Memory-Guided Prompt Refinement.
Table 6: Ablation of auxiliary vision-language models.

Figure 3: Illustration of Memory-Guided Prompt Refinement. Sparse anchors and dense priors produce sparse and dense features, which are fused into memory-guided prompt embeddings and decoded together with the detector’s original prompts.
